party_actions.rule: When a brown lesion has an asymmetric overall shape with irregular peripheral projections AND contains dark brown-to-black areas unevenly distributed within the lesion body AND the border transitions vary from sharp cutoff on one side to gradual fading on another, classify as Melanoma.
preconditions: The lesion has an asymmetric shape with uneven peripheral projections — small finger-like or jagged extensions of pigment that differ from one side to the other
Dark brown-to-black blotches or clumps are distributed unevenly within the lesion, concentrated off-center rather than forming a symmetric gradient
The border character varies around the lesion: some edges show sharp, abrupt pigment cutoff while other edges show gradual, smudged fading into surrounding skin
party_actions.rationale: Benign melanocytic nevi typically show symmetric structure with uniform border transitions and centrally-located darkest pigment. When dark pigment blotches are eccentric, the overall shape is asymmetric with irregular projections, and the border transitions differ around the circumference, these are hallmarks of disordered melanocytic growth in melanoma. The PUPIL likely focused on the globular internal structures and missed the asymmetric distribution and heterogeneous border behavior that distinguish this from a benign globular nevus.

party_actions.validator_observations: The lesion appears relatively symmetric and oval/round in shape. The peripheral projections (small dot-like extensions) are fairly evenly distributed around the border, resembling a somewhat regular 'corona' pattern rather than asymmetric jagged projections. The dark brown areas are centrally concentrated with a fairly symmetric gradient toward the periphery. The border shows a relatively consistent character around most of the lesion — predominantly gradual fading with peripheral dots/globules — without a clear contrast between sharp cutoff on one side and gradual fading on another. The pre-conditions for asymmetric shape with uneven peripheral projections and variable border character are not clearly met.

rounds.validator_observations: The lesion appears relatively symmetric and oval/round in shape. The pre-conditions for asymmetric shape with uneven peripheral projections and variable border character are not clearly met.

party_actions.rule: When a brown lesion has a relatively symmetric oval shape with a 'corona' pattern of peripheral dots/globules evenly distributed around the border AND dark brown areas are centrally concentrated with a gradient toward the periphery, classify as Melanoma.
preconditions: The lesion has a relatively symmetric oval shape with small dot-like extensions forming a 'corona' pattern of peripheral dots/globules distributed around the border
Dark brown areas are centrally concentrated with a gradient fading toward the periphery, and the border shows predominantly gradual fading with peripheral dots/globules
The central area contains dark brown-to-black pigment that is denser than the surrounding peripheral zone, creating a two-toned or multi-toned appearance within the lesion
party_actions.rationale: A peripheral corona of dots/globules surrounding a centrally concentrated dark pigment zone suggests radial growth phase activity at the lesion periphery, which is a hallmark of melanoma. The peripheral globules represent nests of melanocytes extending beyond the main tumor body, and the central dark concentration reflects deeper or denser melanocytic proliferation. This pattern differs from benign nevi, which typically show a uniform pigment network without a distinct corona of peripheral structures or marked central-peripheral pigment disparity.

party_actions.validator_observations: The lesion does show a roughly oval shape with peripheral dots/globules forming a corona-like pattern around the border. However, the central pigmentation does not clearly show a denser dark brown-to-black central area creating a distinct two-toned gradient toward the periphery. The pigment distribution appears relatively uniform or even slightly lighter centrally, with the darker pigment concentrated more in a mid-zone ring rather than a classic center-to-periphery gradient. The pre-condition requiring centrally concentrated dark brown-to-black pigment denser than the peripheral zone is not clearly met. This pattern is more consistent with a Reed nevus (pigmented spindle cell nevus) than melanoma.

rounds.validator_observations: The lesion does show a roughly oval shape with peripheral dots/globules forming a corona-like pattern around the border. However, the central pigmentation does not clearly show a denser dark brown-to-black central area creating a distinct two-toned gradient toward the periphery. The pigment distribution appears relatively uniform or even slightly lighter centrally, with the darker pigment concentrated more in a mid-zone ring rather than a classic center-to-periphery gradient. The pre-condition requiring centrally concentrated dark brown-to-black pigment denser than the peripheral zone is not clearly met. This pattern is more consistent with a Reed nevus (pigmented spindle cell nevus) than melanoma.

party_actions.rule: When a roughly oval brown lesion shows peripheral dots/globules forming a corona-like pattern around the border AND darker pigment is concentrated in a mid-zone ring rather than centrally, classify as Melanoma.
preconditions: The lesion has a roughly oval shape with peripheral dots/globules forming a corona-like pattern around the border
Darker pigment is concentrated in a mid-zone ring rather than being uniformly distributed or centrally concentrated, creating an uneven pigment architecture
The lesion contains multiple shades of brown with areas that appear slightly lighter centrally compared to the mid-zone ring
party_actions.rationale: A mid-zone ring of darker pigment with a relatively lighter center and peripheral corona of dots/globules suggests disordered melanocyte proliferation at different depths and radial positions. This non-uniform, ring-like pigment distribution pattern — where the darkest pigment sits between the center and periphery rather than following a simple central-to-peripheral gradient — is an atypical architectural feature that favors melanoma over a simple benign nevus, which typically shows uniform or centrally darkest pigmentation.

party_actions.validator_observations: The lesion is roughly oval in shape with clearly visible peripheral dots/globules forming a corona-like pattern around the border. The darker pigment appears concentrated in a mid-zone ring rather than being centrally dominant, with the center appearing slightly lighter. Multiple shades of brown are visible, with an uneven pigment architecture consistent with the described pattern.

rounds.validator_observations: The lesion is roughly oval in shape with clearly visible peripheral dots/globules forming a corona-like pattern around the border. The darker pigment appears concentrated in a mid-zone ring rather than being centrally dominant, with the center appearing slightly lighter. Multiple shades of brown are visible, with an uneven pigment architecture consistent with the described pattern.

final_rule.rule: When a roughly oval brown lesion shows peripheral dots/globules forming a corona-like pattern around the border AND darker pigment is concentrated in a mid-zone ring rather than centrally, classify as Melanoma.
preconditions: The lesion has a roughly oval shape with peripheral dots/globules forming a corona-like pattern around the border
Darker pigment is concentrated in a mid-zone ring rather than being uniformly distributed or centrally concentrated, creating an uneven pigment architecture
The lesion contains multiple shades of brown with areas that appear slightly lighter centrally compared to the mid-zone ring
final_rule.rationale: A mid-zone ring of darker pigment with a relatively lighter center and peripheral corona of dots/globules suggests disordered melanocyte proliferation at different depths and radial positions. This non-uniform, ring-like pigment distribution pattern — where the darkest pigment sits between the center and periphery rather than following a simple central-to-peripheral gradient — is an atypical architectural feature that favors melanoma over a simple benign nevus, which typically shows uniform or centrally darkest pigmentation.

party_actions.rule: When a lesion shows scattered granular gray-brown dots and blotches distributed irregularly across the lesion, multiple white-pink structureless patches interspersed among pigmented areas, and an overall chaotic mix of at least 3 distinct color tones (tan, dark brown, gray, and white-pink), classify as Melanoma.
preconditions: Numerous fine granular gray-brown dots and pepper-like specks scattered irregularly across the lesion, not forming any organized or repeating pattern
Multiple white-to-pink structureless patches (paler than surrounding skin) interspersed within the lesion, appearing as irregular pale islands among pigmented areas
At least 3 distinct color tones visible across the lesion — including tan/light brown, dark brown, gray, and white-pink — distributed in a disorganized, asymmetric arrangement
party_actions.rationale: The pupil likely saw the relatively flat, diffuse pigmentation and interpreted it as a benign nevus. However, this lesion displays classic signs of melanoma with regression: the scattered granular gray-brown dots indicate melanophages (pigment remnant), the white-pink structureless areas represent fibrosis replacing tumor, and the chaotic multi-tonal color distribution without any symmetric or organized pigment network is incompatible with a benign nevus. A benign nevus would show a uniform or gradually fading pigment network, not this disordered granular pigment mixed with pale regression zones.

party_actions.validator_observations: The image shows: (1) numerous fine granular gray-brown dots and pepper-like specks scattered irregularly without organized pattern; (2) multiple white-to-pink structureless patches visible as pale islands interspersed among pigmented areas; (3) at least 3-4 distinct color tones including tan/light brown, dark brown, gray, and white-pink distributed in a disorganized, asymmetric arrangement. All three pre-conditions are clearly met.

All three pre-conditions are clearly met.

final_rule.rationale: The pupil likely saw the relatively flat, diffuse pigmentation and interpreted it as a benign nevus. However, this lesion displays classic signs of melanoma with regression: the scattered granular gray-brown dots indicate melanophages (pigment remnant), the white-pink structureless areas represent fibrosis replacing tumor, and the chaotic multi-tonal color distribution without any symmetric or organized pigment network is incompatible with a benign nevus. A benign nevus would show a uniform or gradually fading pigment network, not this disordered granular pigment mixed with pale regression zones.

party_actions.rule: When a small dark lesion shows a central gray-blue or gray-white structureless zone surrounded by an irregular brown rim, and the lesion sits on sun-damaged skin with scattered background solar lentigines, classify as Melanoma.
preconditions: The lesion contains a central zone that is gray-blue or gray-white (lighter than the surrounding pigmented rim), appearing as a pale area within the darker border — indicating regression or loss of pigment in the center
The brown pigment at the lesion periphery is unevenly distributed, with variable shades of dark brown to tan forming an asymmetric ring or partial ring around the central pale area
The surrounding skin shows signs of chronic sun damage: scattered faint tan-yellow patches (solar lentigines) and fine pink-red linear vessels visible in the background
party_actions.rationale: The PUPIL likely saw a small, relatively round lesion and defaulted to benign nevus. However, the gray-white/gray-blue central area within the lesion is a hallmark of regression — where the body's immune response has partially destroyed melanoma cells, leaving fibrosis (white) or melanophages (gray-blue). A benign nevus does not typically show this central depigmented gray zone surrounded by an irregular dark brown rim. The combination of regression features in a lesion on chronically sun-damaged skin (evidenced by background solar lentigines and telangiectasias) strongly favors melanoma, particularly in older patients. The asymmetric distribution of brown pigment around the periphery further supports malignancy over a uniform benign nevus.

party_actions.validator_observations: The lesion shows a central gray-blue/gray-white structureless zone that is lighter than the surrounding brown pigmented rim, consistent with regression. The peripheral brown pigment is unevenly distributed, forming an asymmetric ring with variable shades of dark brown to tan. The surrounding skin displays scattered faint tan-yellow patches consistent with solar lentigines, and fine pink-red vessels are visible in the background, indicating chronic sun damage. All three pre-conditions appear to be met.

rounds.validator_observations: The lesion shows a central gray-blue/gray-white structureless zone that is lighter than the surrounding brown pigmented rim, consistent with regression. party_actions.rule: When a brown lesion shows dark brown-black granular clumps clustered asymmetrically in one portion of the lesion, with a separate zone of lighter diffuse tan-brown pigment fading irregularly at the periphery, classify as Melanoma.
preconditions: Dark brown-to-black granular or clumped dots/globules are concentrated unevenly in one area of the lesion (e.g., upper half) rather than distributed symmetrically throughout
A lighter tan-brown diffuse pigment zone extends irregularly from the darker center, with borders that fade unevenly and are not sharply demarcated from surrounding skin
The overall lesion lacks a single uniform color — at least three distinguishable shades (dark brown-black clumps, medium brown, light tan haze) are visible in an asymmetric arrangement
party_actions.rationale: Benign nevi typically show symmetric pigment distribution with a uniform or gradually darkening center and well-defined borders. The combination of asymmetrically clustered dark granules, multiple color shades, and an irregular diffuse peripheral haze suggests disordered melanocytic proliferation characteristic of melanoma. The PUPIL likely focused on the overall brown color and small size without recognizing the asymmetric internal structure and irregular border fading that are red flags for malignancy.

party_actions.validator_observations: The lesion shows dark brown-to-black granular clumps concentrated in the upper-central portion asymmetrically. A lighter, diffuse tan-brown zone extends irregularly outward with borders that fade unevenly into the surrounding skin. At least three distinguishable shades are present: dark brown-black clumps centrally, medium brown in the body of the lesion, and a light tan haze at the periphery — arranged asymmetrically. All three pre-conditions appear to be met.

A lighter, diffuse tan-brown zone extends irregularly outward with borders that fade unevenly into the surrounding skin.
